msg: assignment discards 'const' qualifier from pointer target type (CType(CPointer(CUnsigned(CLong())),true,false,false) = CType(CPointer(CUnsigned(CLong())),false,false,true))
reasoning: The warning fires only under presence condition def(CONFIG_NUMA)&def(CONFIG_PCI)&!def(const). The '!def(const)' term reveals the issue: the family-based analysis tool is treating the C keyword 'const' as if it were a preprocessor macro/configuration option. In any real Linux kernel build, 'const' is always a language keyword; there is no #define that enables or disables it. The 'assignment discards const qualifier' warning is a pure artifact of the tool's handling of the const qualifier in its configuration-lifted type system — it does not correspond to a real type error in any actual build configuration. False positive.
